steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks which physical trait allows small cats to purr. The answer must come directly from the passage's stated mechanism, not general knowledge.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — The ligament attaching the hyoid bone to the rusty-spotted cat's skull.: the passage says small cats have a rigid hyoid, not a flexible one. This describes big cats. ✗
  • Choice B — The relative size of the rusty-spotted cat's body and throat.: the larynx vibrates in both big and small cats; it is a shared component, not the trait that distinguishes purring ability. ✗
  • Choice C — The rusty-spotted cat's rigid hyoid bone.: the passage states the rigid hyoid rumbles when the larynx vibrates, directly producing the purr. This is the key distinguishing trait. ✓
  • Choice D — The shape of the rusty-spotted cat's larynx.: larynx size is not mentioned as the mechanism; the passage focuses on the hyoid. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
